qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded request-body buffering in default body-token extraction enables memory DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets request-body buffering in RequestContext.Body() and body-parameter token extraction, while the advisory fixes authorization bypass from raw-versus-normalized path handling and dot-segment rejection during rule evaluation.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's path-normalization authorization-bypass root cause.
evidence: The advisory and fix center on request-path normalization and dot-segment rejection in rule evaluation, including changes to internal/handler/requestcontext/extract_url.go, internal/rules/rule_executor_impl.go, and internal/x/urlx/path.go.

The only real qualifying final finding in the session manifest is request-body-buffering-dos; it concerns full request-body buffering in internal/handler/requestcontext/request_context.go and BodyParameterExtractStrategy-based token extraction.

That finding does not describe raw-path rule matching, downstream path normalization mismatch, dot-segment acceptance, or an authorization decision being made on a different path than the downstream service processes.
